late_blight.symptoms: Dark, water-soaked spots on leaves that turn brown/black; white mold growth on the undersides of leaves in humid conditions; dark lesions on fruit or tubers.
organic_treatments: Copper octanoate (OMRI listed soap-based copper fungicide)
Bacillus subtilis strain QST 713 bio-fungicide spray
cultural_practices: Prune the bottom 12 inches of leaves to prevent soil-splash inoculation.
Ensure wide spacing between plants (at least 24-36 inches) to maximize airflow.
Use drip irrigation exclusively to keep foliage dry.
Destroy all infected plant residue immediately; do not compost.
late_blight.eco_status: Approved under Universal Design for Agriculture (UDA) criteria.

wheat_rust.symptoms: Yellowish-brown or orange pustules on leaves, leaf sheaths, and stems, resembling rust spots.
organic_treatments: Wettable sulfur spray (early preventative application only)
Cold-pressed neem oil extract (1% concentration for early stages)
cultural_practices: Plant rust-resistant crop cultivars approved by local extension offices.
Implement a 3-year crop rotation program with non-cereal crops (e.g., legumes).
Sow crops early in the season to avoid peak spore release times.
wheat_rust.eco_status: Approved under Universal Design for Agriculture (UDA) criteria.

powdery_mildew.symptoms: White, powdery spots or patches on the upper surface of leaves, stems, and buds; leaves may yellow and curl.
organic_treatments: Potassium bicarbonate spray (3 tablespoons per gallon of water with a mild soap surfactant)
Dilute milk spray (40% milk, 60% water - acts as a natural fungicide under sunlight)
cultural_practices: Locate plantings in full sun areas where possible.
Thin out foliage inside the canopy to increase light penetration and airflow.
Avoid overhead watering during evening hours.
powdery_mildew.eco_status: Approved under Universal Design for Agriculture (UDA) criteria.

citrus_greening.symptoms: Asymmetrical yellowing of leaves (mottling); small, narrow, upright leaves; small, lopsided fruit that remains green at the bottom and has a bitter taste.
organic_treatments: Kaolin clay spray (creates a physical barrier against Asian Citrus Psyllid vectors)
Horticultural mineral oils (suffocates vector eggs and nymphs)
cultural_practices: Plant only certified disease-free nursery stock.
Release natural predators like parasitic wasps (Tamarixia radiata) to control psyllid vectors.
Apply specialized micronutrient sprays to boost tree immunity and sustain fruit yield.
citrus_greening.eco_status: Approved under Universal Design for Agriculture (UDA) criteria.
warnings: No direct cure exists. Focus is strictly vector control and host plant health preservation.

leaf_spot.symptoms: Small, circular spots with dark brown margins and grey centers, appearing first on lower leaves; yellow halos around spots.
cultural_practices: Maintain a 3-year crop rotation cycle away from Solanaceous plants.
Apply organic mulch (straw or paper) around the base of plants to act as a barrier against soil spores.
Thoroughly clean stakes, cages, and gardening tools at the end of the season using a 10% bleach solution.
leaf_spot.eco_status: Approved under Universal Design for Agriculture (UDA) criteria.
